test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
As used in the text, what does the word “display” most nearly mean?
The following text is adapted from John Matheus’s 1926 short story *Mr. Bradford Teaches Sunday School*. Mr. Bradford is driving through the countryside in Florida.
The moss in the towering water oaks had become enlivened with a verdant sheen of silver and hung like festoons of carnival or like funeral decorations for the mourning of the dead. The pine green was resplendent. The bald cypresses spread themselves along the water courses while the willows wept as they always did. Mr. Bradford was conscious for this gorgeous <u>display</u> of nature.
question_text: As used in the text, what does the word “display” most nearly mean?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Spectacle

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reproduction

choices.label: C
choices.text: Pretentiousness

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disguise

Mexican textile artist Victoria Villasana weaves stories of triumph, using her unique method of applying colorful yarn to photographs of people. In some works, Villasana focuses on celebrating cultural icons who are people of color, as she does in her depiction of activist Ryu Gwansun. <u>However, in other works, Villasana honors ordinary people, as she does in her captivating portrayal of a young girl sitting on a sidewalk.</u> Villasana sees both of these approaches as ways of depicting the power and interconnectedness of all people.

choices.label: D

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Based on the texts, how would Odom and colleagues (Text 2) most likely respond to the view of birdsong presented in Text 1?
Text 1
For decades ornithologists assumed that if they saw a singing Bell’s vireo—a bird species found in temperate North America—they must be observing a male. That’s because birdsong has long been considered a male trait; researchers have argued that singing enables males to attract mates and claim territory.
Text 2
Recent evidence shows that a female Bell’s vireo is as capable of song as a male is. In fact, Kara J. Odom and colleagues found evidence of female song in 71% of the 323 species they examined. They claim that the historical mischaracterization of birdsong as a male trait is largely the result of bias: much of the research ornithologists have carried out has been near universities in the temperate northern hemisphere, where female birdsong is less common than it is in the tropics.
question_text: Based on the texts, how would Odom and colleagues (Text 2) most likely respond to the view of birdsong presented in Text 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They would suggest that it reflected a tendency to study male birds rather than female birds.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They would claim that other factors than mate attraction and territorial defense have driven the evolution of singing in male birds.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They would underscore that male songbirds in temperate zones are likely using their songs for different purposes than are male songbirds in the tropics.

choices.label: D
choices.text: They would argue that it was influenced by the kinds of study sites researchers tended to select.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Based on the text, what can most reasonably be concluded about krill consumption among sei and northern right whales?
Like all species of baleen whales, the sei whale feeds on tiny creatures known as krill by filtering water through bristlelike keratin structures called baleen plates. In this way, baleen whales can eat up to 30 percent of their total mass per day. And while no one would call the sei whale small—it can have a mass as high as 25,000 kg—it is one of the smaller baleen whales and is much smaller than the northern right whale, which can weigh a whopping 80,000 kg and consume as much as 24,000 kg krill per day.
question_text: Based on the text, what can most reasonably be concluded about krill consumption among sei and northern right whales?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The northern right whale is able to eat more krill per day than the sei whale is.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both the sei whale and the northern right whale can eat as much as 24,000 kg of krill per day.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The quantities of krill consumed by northern right whales has made it difficult for sei whales to find sufficient food.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Most baleen whales include krill in their diets, but the sei whale is less likely than the northern right whale to do so.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
Wenheng Zhang and colleagues have explored how convergent evolution—a phenomenon that occurs when the same trait evolves independently in two reproductively separate lineages—can result from a genetic mechanism shared by both lineages. Meanwhile, Armin P. Moczek and colleagues have investigated how convergence occur through different genetic mechanisms, but the relative prevalence of convergence through shared and different genetic processes is still poorly understood. This motivated biologists Delber A. Green II and Cassandra G. Extavour to evaluate both types of convergence in a single study for their 2012 paper.
question_text: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Before Green and Extavour’s study, convergent evolution was assumed to require a shared genetic mechanism between two lineages that share the trait.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Green and Extavour’s study was conducted using data from the studies by Zhang and colleagues and Moczek and colleagues.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Green and Extavour’s study addresses convergent evolution more comprehensively than the studies by Zhang and colleagues and Moczek and colleagues do.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both the study by Zhang and colleagues and that by Green and Extavour compare convergence through shared genetic mechanism to convergence through different genetic mechanisms.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?
US Hydroelectric Power Plants, 2019
A run-of-river hydroelectric power plant, as the name suggests, uses the natural flow of a water source to generate electricity but is unable to start or stop that flow through its generators. In contrast, a peaking hydroelectric power plant (used when demand for electricity peaks) controls the flow of water through its generators: starting flow when demand is high enough, stopping it when demand is too low, and otherwise regulating it to keep pace with changing electricity needs. Although peaking plants do not typically operate continuously as run-of-river plants do, peaking plants can generate more megawatt hours of power per year (MWh/yr) than some run-of-river plants. For example, the ______
question_text: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?

choices.label: A
choices.text: average power generated annually by the J. Woodruff plant is higher than that generated by any of the run-of-river plants in the table.

choices.label: B
choices.text: J. Woodruff plant, which is a peaking plant, has more generators than any of the other plant in the table.

choices.label: C
choices.text: average power generated annually by the Norway plant is higher than that generated by the Superior Falls plant.

choices.label: D
choices.text: run-of-river plant with the highest average annual power generation in the table generated more electricity than the peaking plant with the highest annual power generation in the table.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best describes data in the table that support the researcher’s claim?
It is common for freshwater lakes near or above a latitude of 45 o north of the equator, like Lake Mjosa in Norway, to accumulate surface ice in winter. The amount and duration of ice depends on many factors, including local weather conditions as well as the lake’s depth, volume, and surface area, but a climate researcher claims that some lakes in these latitudes have seen a decline in the duration of ice between the early 1980s and the mid-2000s.
question_text: Which choice best describes data in the table that support the researcher’s claim?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Kalmarinjarvi had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980-81.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Kalmarinjarvi is at a higher latitude than Mirror Lake and typically had fewer days of ice per winter than Mirror Lake did.

choices.label: C

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lake Neusiedl had more days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it had in the winter of 1980-81.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which finding, if true, would most directly weaken the hypothesis presented in the text?
Many pastes contain copper oxide nanoparticles (CuO-NPs) which can leach into waterways and soils via wastewater. In a 2012 study, Superb K. Misra and colleagues found that CuO-NPs can accumulate in the bodies of great pond snails (*Lymnaea stagnalis*). While bioaccumulation of manufactured nanoparticles may be inherently worrisome, it has been hypothesized that CuO-NP bioaccumulation in invertebrates like *L. stagnalis* could serve a valuable proxy role, obviating the need for manufacturers to conduct costly and intrusive sampling of vertebrate species—such as African clawed frogs (*Xenopus laevis*), commonly used in regulatory compliance testing—for nanoparticle bioaccumulation, as environmental protection laws currently require.
question_text: Which finding, if true, would most directly weaken the hypothesis presented in the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When *L. stagnalis* and *X. laevis* are exposed to similar levels of CuO-NPs, concentrations of CuO-NPs in animals of both species show little variation from individual to individual.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The rate of CuO-NP uptake in *L. stagnalis* differs from the rate of CuO-NP uptake in *X. laevis* in a way that is not yet well understood by researchers.

choices.label: C
choices.text: *L. stagnalis* has been shown to accumulate several other types of manufactured nanoparticles in addition to CuO-NPs, whereas *X. laevis* has been shown to accumulate only CuO-NPs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Compared with *X. laevis*, *L. stagnalis* can accumulate detectable CuO-NP concentrations with significantly fewer negative effects.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which finding, if true, would most directly challenge the second research team’s conclusion?
Callie W. Babbitt, Hema Madaka, and colleagues assembled a database of materials used in consumer electronics by studying products in the lab and by gathering data from similar product studies. The team gave each of these studies a rating for level of traceability (with a higher rating for clearer descriptions of procedures) and for category consistency (with a higher rating for using materials categories more closely aligned with the categories in the team's database). Based on these ratings, a second research team concluded that the methodology was better explained in a study by Lutz Stobbe than it was in a study by Martin Streicher-Porte and colleagues.
question_text: Which finding, if true, would most directly challenge the second research team’s conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The study by Stobbe had a lower traceability than the study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues did.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The study by Stobbe had a high consistency rating and a high traceability rating.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The study by Stobbe had a lower consistency rating than the study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues did.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues had a medium consistency rating and a medium traceability rating.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Arthurian legends (tales related to the character of King Arthur) derive from many often contradictory sources, such as *Preiddeu Annwfn*, composed around 900, and *Perceval, the Story of the Grail* from around 1181. Sir Thomas Malory's 15th-century text *Le Morte d'Arthur* was an attempt to compile these stories into a coherent narrative. Many of Malory's sources derive from Geoffrey of Monmouth's *History of the Kings of Britain*, written in the 1130s. While neither *History* nor any works that predate it mention Arthur's famous Round Table at which his knights assembled, *Le Morte d'Arthur* does, suggesting that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Geoffrey of Monmouth's accounts of Arthurian legends in *History* are more similar overall in content to the accounts in *Perceval, the Story of the Grail* than they are to the accounts in *Le Morte d'Arthur*.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Malory encountered the Round Table in a source that Geoffrey of Monmouth was not familiar with when writing his *History*.

choices.label: C
choices.text: *Le Morte d'Arthur* is more historically accurate than *History*, because *Perceval, the Story of the Grail* had not been written when Geoffrey of Monmouth was writing his work.

choices.label: D
choices.text: when a version of an Arthurian legend contradicted the version in *History*, Malory preferred to include Geoffrey of Monmouth’s version in *Le Morte d'Arthur*.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
In June of 1987, South Korea liberalized its stock market, meaning that it began allowing foreign individuals and businesses to invest money in South Korean companies. This was part of a wave of stock market liberalizations from the mid-1980s through the mid-1990s—Brazil in 1988, Venezuela in 1990, and so on. In an analysis of economic data from 1976 to 1993, Ross Levine and Sara Zervos found that liberalization did not lead to enduring increases in investment in companies based in countries that liberalized. Peter Blair Henry, however, found that, on average, investment in companies in liberalized countries increased significantly in the three years following liberalization. Taken together, these results suggest that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: investment growth is likely to be more consistent in countries that liberalize than in countries that do not.

choices.label: B
choices.text: it typically takes at least three years for companies to benefit from government policies allowing foreign investment, but governments rarely maintain such policies for that long.

choices.label: C
choices.text: economists' expectations about the effect of liberalization on investment were largely correct.

choices.label: D
choices.text: companies based in countries that begin allowing foreign investment will probably see short-term increases in investment but that these gains are unlikely to last.

• The painting *Lightness of Being* (24*30in) depicts a young man with his arms outstretched floating on the right side of the canvas.
• *Lost in the Shuffle* (36*24in) depicts two young men with their arms outstretched floating in the bottom left and upper right corners of the canvas.

choices.text: In Rawles’s painting *Lost in the Shuffle*, two young men are depicted in the bottom left and upper right corners of the canvas.

choices.label: D
choices.text: While the number of figures may differ, constant among Rawles’s hyperrealistic works is the calm mood that paintings evoke.

test_name: August 2023 Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize when she collected both of the samples?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Ynés Mexía was a Mexican American botanist.
• Between 1917 and 1938, she collected over 150,000 botanical samples throughout the Americas.
• She collected a sample of *Trixis antimenorrhoea* in Minas Gerais, Brazil, on June 26, 1930.
• She collected a sample of *Stephanomeria thurberi* in Chihuahua, Mexico, on May 27, 1929.
• These specimens are members of the Asteraceae family.
• They can now be viewed online at the C. V. Starr Virtual Herbarium.
question_text: Which choice most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize when she collected both of the samples?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Thousands of botanical samples collected by Ynés Mexía can now be found in one place: online at the C. V. Starr Virtual Herbarium.

choices.label: B
choices.text: While both specimens collected by Ynés Mexía are members of the same family, *Trixis antimenorrhoea* was found in Minas Gerais and *Stephanomeria thurberi* was found in Chihuahua.

choices.label: C
choices.text: On June 26 in 1930, Ynés Mexía added a new specimen to her growing collection of botanical samples: *Trixis antimenorrhoea* of the Asteraceae family.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ynés Mexía collected a sample of *Trixis antimenorrhoea* in June of 1930, after collecting *Stephanomeria thurberi* in May of 1929.
